title: Parliamentary and Cabinet Committees
focus: Discusses the Estimates Committee, Consultative Committees, and the Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS).
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 013]
Note: Ad Hoc Committee set up by Parliament and Parliamentary Department Related Standing Committee review independent regulators in sectors like telecommunications, insurance electricity, etc.
Details of Committees Recently in the News
Estimates Committee (Completed 75 years since formation)
- Largest of three financial committees (others: Committee on Public Undertakings, and Public Accounts Committee).
- 30 Lok Sabha members; Ministers not eligible.
- Chairperson appointed by Speaker.
- Tenure: 1 year.
- Functions: Suggest alternative policies for efficiency; examine whether expenditure is well-utilised within policy limits.
Consultative Committees (CCs)
- Function under Ministry of Parliamentary Affairs (Allocation of Business Rules, 1961).
- Constituted after Lok Sabha elections/as required.
- Voluntary membership: 10–30 MPs.
- One MP can be member of only one CC.
- MPs from both Houses nominated by Ministry of Parliamentary Affairs.
- Objective: Informal consultation between Government and MPs on policies/programmes.
Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS)
- Chair: Prime Minister. CCS is one of eight Cabinet Committees.
- Members: Ministers of Defence, Home, Finance, External Affairs. Defence chiefs/senior officials may attend.
- Functions: National security, defence, internal security, major security appointments, foreign affairs with security implications.
- Cabinet Committees: Extra-constitutional; provided under Transaction of Business Rules, 1961.
- Major committees (Political Affairs, Economic Affairs, Appointments) chaired by PM; Parliamentary Affairs Committee chaired by Defence Minister.
Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC)
- Composition: PM (Chairperson) + Home Minister.
- Responsibilities: Senior government appointments (Secretaries, AS, JS); top posts in PSUs, banks, insurance companies, and autonomous bodies.
1.8. LOCAL BODIES
1.8.1. PANCHAYAT ADVANCEMENT INDEX (PAI)
